items.briefing:
A phishing toolkit called **Kali365**, previously flagged by the FBI, is expanding its reach and being used to craft convincing fake login pages to steal credentials. Separately, a malware campaign called **WeedHack** has infected over 116,000 systems through unofficial Minecraft downloads — a reminder that gaming software from untrusted sources can carry serious risks, particularly for households with younger users.

Google's Global Signal Exchange has also upgraded its fraud detection platform, which may help improve scam identification across digital services used in Australia.

**Practical tip:** Remind staff and family members to only download games and software from official sources, and to be cautious of any unexpected login pages asking for usernames or passwords.

featured.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
featured.quote: AI systems can be socially engineered to compromise high-profile accounts, exposing critical platform security gaps.
